summary: Chapter 14 of Tom Mitchell's Machine Learning textbook reviews key ideas from a machine learning course. It frames ML as the study of systems that improve with experience, defined by task, performance metric, and training experience, and presents core perspectives and foundational results in the field.
bullets: A learning problem is defined by the triple <T, P, E>: task, performance measure, and training experience
ML can be viewed through four perspectives: optimization, probabilistic inference, parametric programming, and evolutionary search
No Free Lunch: generalization beyond training data is impossible without prior assumptions or inductive bias
Errors in learned functions come from three sources: bias, variance, and unavoidable (irreducible) error
Overfitting occurs when training error underestimates true error, signaling poor generalization
